title: 10 best herbal tinctures for swollen feet

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The astringent nature of witch hazel constricts blood vessels, reducing swelling and relieving discomfort caused by fluid buildup in the feet. Additionally, the herbal extract's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections and promote healthy skin, further alleviating swollen foot symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from aching, puffy feet, making it a trusted solution for many.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties can help reduce swelling by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup in the feet.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Witch Hazel contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate pain, redness, and inflammation caused by swollen feet.
Antimicrobial properties: Witch Hazel's antimicrobial properties can help prevent bacterial or fungal infections that may contribute to swollen feet, such as athlete's foot or fungal nails.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help reduce inflammation and swelling by constricting blood vessels and promoting the removal of excess fluid from the affected area.

Hamamelitannin: Hamamelitannin, a specific type of tannin found in Hamamelis virginiana, has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote healing in swollen feet.

Astringent phenolics (e.g. quercetin): Astringent phenolics, such as quercetin, help to reduce swelling and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and promoting the removal of excess fluid from the affected area, while also reducing oxidative stress and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties to help reduce swelling and promote healing.
Barks: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
Roots: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties to help reduce swelling and promote urination to alleviate fluid retention.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in early morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Clean the plant material by gently shaking off excess debris to prevent contamination of the tincture.
Combine 1 part of the plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:2 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow the tincture to mature and infuse.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture's active compound, calendula, penetrates deep into the skin to reduce swelling and discomfort by constricting blood vessels and preventing further inflammation. Additionally, calendula's soothing effects calm irritated skin, alleviating pain and stiffness associated with swollen feet. Regular use of pot marigold tinctures can provide long-term relief from swollen feet, promoting overall foot health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by inflammation in the feet, making it an effective natural remedy for conditions like arthritis and gout.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tinctures help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy circulation to alleviate swollen feet.

Venotonic: Pot marigold's venotonic properties help improve blood flow and reduce swelling in the veins, which is particularly beneficial for individuals who experience frequent or chronic swelling in their feet due to poor circulation or varicose veins.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and relieve swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These polyphenols exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate swelling and promote the healing of damaged tissues in the feet.

Flavonoids (specifically, luteolin and apigenin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce swelling, soothe pain, and promote the healing of wounds in the feet.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the feet.
Leaves: Leaves are often used for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in swollen feet.
Roots: Roots are sometimes used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and combat infections in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated and before heat of day sets in.
Rinse fresh flowers gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris from harvesting process.
Use a mortar and pestle to finely chop 1 part of fresh flowers with 1 part of vodka to create a mixture.
Combine chopped flower mixture with 2 parts of glycerin and 2 parts of distilled water in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to extract active compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compounds present in aloe vera, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce inflammation and swelling by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, aloe's ability to improve blood circulation and break down excess fluids can also contribute to relief from swollen feet. By applying an herbal aloe tincture topically or taking it orally, individuals can experience reduced discomfort, improved mobility, and enhanced overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling by decreasing prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation in the body.
Antioxidant property: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to inflammation and swelling in the feet.

Wound-healing property: Aloe vera's gel contains enzymes that promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing scar tissue formation, which can help reduce swelling and improve overall foot health.
remedy_constituents: Phenolics: Help reduce inflammation and swelling by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress in the affected area.
Glucomannans: Help reduce fluid retention and swelling by inhibiting the absorption of water and electrolytes in the body, thereby reducing the accumulation of fluid in the feet.

Triterpenoids (specifically, aloin): Help reduce inflammation and pain associated with swollen feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Aloe vera's medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling.
Stems: Some tinctures use the stems, which also contain anti-inflammatory compounds and can help soothe swollen feet.
Barks: The barks of the Aloe vera plant contain flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and can be used to make tinctures for swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 fresh aloe vera leaves of 5-7 inches long with thick fleshy skin for tincture preparation.
Chop the harvested aloe vera leaves into small pieces weighing 1 pound for extraction.
Steep the chopped aloe vera pieces in 2 quarts of 80% ethanol at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a glass bottle for tincture separation.
Store the herbal aloe vera tincture in a cool dark place for 2 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tincture's natural ingredients, such as Echinacea pallida, work to soothe and calm the affected area, allowing for a reduction in inflammation and pain. Additionally, Kansas coneflower has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can also contribute to alleviating swelling and promoting overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Ant-inflammatory compounds: Echinacein, a key compound found in Kansas Coneflower, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet.
Antioxidant activity: The tincture's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin and underlying tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.
Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower has been traditionally used to boost the immune system, which may help reduce the severity of swollen feet caused by infections or allergic reactions.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These help reduce inflammation and pain associated with swollen feet by modulating the body's immune response and reducing the production of inflammatory compounds.

Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate swelling, pain, and discomfort in the feet by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation.

Flavonoids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to reduced swelling and pain in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting blood vessel health.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of echinacoside, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They may contribute to the tincture's overall anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Stems: Some herbalists include stems in the tincture to enhance its overall potency and medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the roots in a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours or with the windows open for 7 days.
Chop 1/2 cup of dried echinacea angustifolia roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a coffee grinder.
Steep the chopped roots in 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The herb contains compounds that inhibit the production of histamine, a chemical responsible for increased blood flow and swelling. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to improve circulation, reducing the likelihood of fluid accumulation in the feet. As a result, using an herbal stinging nettle tincture can provide relief from swollen and tender feet, leaving them feeling soft and comfortable once again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in nettle tincture protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to foot swelling and inflammation.
Vasodilatory effects: Nettle tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve circulation and reduce fluid retention, alleviating swelling and discomfort in the feet.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Urtica dioica, particularly stachydrine, help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids such as quercetin in Urtica dioica have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling, relieve pain, and promote the overall health of the skin in the feet.
Tannins: Tannins in Urtica dioica help reduce swelling by constricting blood vessels and improving circulation, which in turn reduces fluid retention and alleviates discomfort in the feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Urtica dioica for tinctures due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds and bioflavonoids.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are used to make tinctures due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica are used in tinctures to help alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the feet, thanks to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop the harvested urtica dioica into small pieces with a clean sharp knife to release its oils.
Combine 1 part chopped urtica dioica with 2 parts 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with swollen feet because it is a natural diuretic that increases urine production, reducing water retention and alleviating swelling. The tincture's ant-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's potassium-rich composition helps to balance sodium levels in the body, further contributing to its decongestant effects on the feet. As a result, herbal dandelion tinctures can provide quick relief from swollen feet, making it an effective and natural solution for this common problem.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tincture's diuretic effect helps to increase urine production, reducing fluid retention and swelling in the feet by flushing out excess water and toxins.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion tincture, such as sesquiterpenes, can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with swollen feet, making it a natural remedy for alleviating discomfort.

Lymphatic stimulant properties: Dandelion's ability to stimulate lymphatic flow helps to promote the removal of excess fluids, toxins, and waste products from the body, which can contribute to reduced swelling in the feet.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: Helps with swollen feet by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the affected area.
Apolactone: Contributes to the reduction of inflammation and swelling in the feet, possibly by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Scopoletin: Exhibits diuretic properties, which can help in reducing fluid retention and swelling in the feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are commonly used to make tinctures for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for swollen feet because they contain compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at the peak of bloom to ensure potency.
Chop the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers finely to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped taraxacum officinale with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 1 minute.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The tincture's boswellic acid content also helps to break down excess fluids that can cause discomfort and stiffness in the feet. Additionally, frankincense has natural astringent properties, which help to constrict blood vessels and improve circulation, further reducing inflammation and promoting healthy foot function. As a result, regular use of herbal frankincense tinctures can provide welcome relief for individuals experiencing swollen or achy feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling in the feet by alleviating pain and inflammation caused by conditions like arthritis, gout, or plantar fasciitis.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of frankincense tincture help protect the feet from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate symptoms of swollen feet and promote tissue repair.

Analgesic: Frankincense tincture's analgesic property provides pain relief for swollen feet by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Beta-boswellic acids: These triterpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen feet.
Acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBA): A potent inhibitor of pro-inflammatory enzymes, AKBA can help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the feet.

Acylated terpenoids (such as boswellic acid): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and alleviate swelling and pain in swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes contain boswellic acids that provide anti-inflammatory benefits and aid in reducing swelling.
Barks: The bark of Boswellia serrata is also used for its anti-inflammatory properties, although it may contain lower concentrations of boswellic acids compared to roots and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or 40-50 grams of dried boswellia serrata roots in the morning after the plant has dried overnight.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar in a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a dark place at room temperature, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with swollen feet because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically as a foot soak or added to a warm bath, the licorice tincture can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by overexertion, poor circulation, or medical conditions such as edema. Additionally, the antispasmodic effects of glycyrrhizin can help relax muscles and ease cramps that often accompany swollen feet. By reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles, licorice tinctures provide natural relief for achy and puffy feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet by inhibiting prostaglandins and leukotrienes.

Vasodilatory properties: Herbal licorice tinctures can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood circulation to the feet, which can help reduce swelling caused by poor circulation or fluid retention.
Antioxidant properties: Licorice root's antioxidant compounds help protect the feet from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, helps with swollen feet by inhibiting the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which is involved in the production of cortisol. This leads to a decrease in water retention and swelling in the feet.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra, such as licopyranocoumarin and licoflavone, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by protecting tissues from oxidative damage and modulating the inflammatory response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are commonly used to make tinctures for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are used in tinctures for swollen feet due to their diuretic properties, which help reduce swelling.

Roots: Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are used in tinctures for swollen feet due to their ability to inhibit 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, an enzyme that converts cortisol into a more potent form, thereby reducing water retention and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind 500 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500 grams of powdered roots with 2 liters of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place then strain and bottle the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its powerful anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, highbush cranberry's diuretic properties help to increase urine output, which can further alleviate excess fluid retention in the feet. As a result, regular use of highbush cranberry tinctures may provide effective relief from swollen, achy feet and ankles.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberry tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort in the feet.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in highbush cranberry tincture, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help alleviate swelling and pain by reducing inflammation in the affected area.
Diuretic properties: Highbush cranberry tincture's diuretic properties can help increase urine production, which can aid in reducing excess fluid retention and alleviating swelling in the feet.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the feet by protecting against oxidative stress and promoting healthy blood vessel function.
Quercetin: As a potent flavonoid, quercetin helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the breakdown of excess fluid.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting healthy blood flow and circulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: They contain anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce swelling.
Roots: They have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh vaccinium macrocarpon berries and clean them thoroughly in cold running water.
Combine the cleaned vaccinium macrocarpon berries with 2 parts of 80-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks to mature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as arctiin and lactucopicrin, work to reduce swelling and inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. This natural remedy also helps to improve circulation, which can become restricted due to poor footwear or prolonged periods of standing or sitting. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, burdock tincture can provide relief from achy, swollen feet, promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock root has anti-inflammatory compounds like polyphenols and sesquiterpenes, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with swollen feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of burdock tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues and contribute to inflammation and swelling in the feet.
Diuretic: Burdock root has a diuretic effect, which can help to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention, thus alleviating swelling in the feet by removing excess fluids from the body.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that can help reduce inflammation and edema in swollen feet by promoting diuresis and improving lymphatic drainage.

Phenolic acids (including caffeic acid and ferulic acid): Phenolic acids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals.

Arctigenin: Arctigenin is a lignan that has anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in feet by inhibiting the activity of inflammatory cells and enzymes, and preventing the formation of new blood vessels that contribute to edema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Arctium lappa are commonly used due to their high content of inulin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are utilized for their diuretic properties, which can help alleviate fluid retention and swelling in the feet.
Seeds: The seeds of Arctium lappa are also used to make tinctures for swollen feet due to their ability to stimulate the lymphatic system and promote drainage, thereby reducing swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-150g of fresh arctium lappa roots and wash them thoroughly with water.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Combine 500g of the dried root pieces with 75% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its rich composition of silica, potassium, and other minerals that help to reduce inflammation and relax the tissues. The tannins present in field horsetail also act as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and discomfort. Diuretic: Field Horsetail's diuretic properties can help increase urine production, reducing fluid retention and alleviating swelling in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica and antioxidants, roots help reduce inflammation and promote circulation to alleviate swollen feet.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes soothe and calm swollen tissues, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 200g of chopped equisetum arvense with 400ml of high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily during steeping period.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils work together to reduce swelling by constricting blood vessels and improving circulation. This allows for the efficient removal of excess fluid and waste products from the affected area, leading to rapid relief from discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet. Vasodilatory effects: Yarrow's flavonoids and other compounds can dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing fluid accumulation that causes swelling in the feet.
Antioxidant activity: Yarrow's antioxidants can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive water retention or poor circulation, which can contribute to swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with swollen feet.
Fruits: Yarrow fruits contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Clean and dry the herb material in a warm oven for 2 hours at 150°f.
Combine 10 parts of the prepared herb with 40% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily, before filtering and transferring to a dark bottle.
Store the tincture at room temperature for up to 2 years or refrigerate for longer shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The bioflavonoids present in elder flowers and berries work synergistically to constrict blood vessels, decreasing fluid accumulation and promoting healthy circulation. Additionally, elder tincture's soothing and calming effects can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with swollen feet, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the symptoms of edema and improving overall foot health.

Antioxidant properties: Elder tincture's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the feet, promoting overall foot health and reducing discomfort.

Vasodilatory properties: Elder tincture's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing fluid retention in the feet, which can alleviate swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with swollen feet.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra leaves and stems according to local regulations and guidelines.
Cleanly chop 2 parts of sambucus nigra with 1 part of 95% ethanol or vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily, at room temperature around 20-22°c.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, squeezing gently to extract as much tincture as possible.
Store the resulting sambucus nigra tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and use within 2 years at a dosage of 30-40 drops thrice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper tinctures helps with swollen feet because it contains compounds that stimulate blood circulation, reducing fluid retention and inflammation. The antiseptic properties of juniper also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from itching and burning sensations often associated with swelling. Additionally, juniper's diuretic properties aid in flushing out excess water and toxins, further alleviating discomfort and promoting overall foot health. Regular use of herbal juniper tinctures can provide long-term relief for swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Juniper tincture increases urine production, which helps to reduce excess fluid retention and swelling in the feet.
Antioxidant property: Juniper's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate foot swelling.
Anti-inflammatory property: The volatile oils present in juniper tincture have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce pain, stiffness, and swelling associated with swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Juniper roots have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with swollen feet.
Barks: Juniper barks contain terpenes that help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate symptoms of swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh juniperus communis leaves or use dried ones in the desired quantity.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine with 60% ethanol or vodka as a solvent.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a glass jar for 14 days stirring daily during peak hours.
Strain the liquid using a coffee filter or cheesecloth and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to two years.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in pink peony tincture help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, leading to swelling and pain in the feet.
Vasodilatory effects: Pink peony's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing fluid retention in the feet, which can contribute to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pink peony plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relieve pain in the feet.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems, contain compounds that help alleviate inflammation and promote healthy blood circulation to reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia officinalis roots in the morning when they are at their most potent and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar to the top with the chopped root mixture and let it steep for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature between 39-45°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The active compounds in the herb, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention. Additionally, St John's Wort has natural astringent qualities that help to tighten skin and improve circulation, further reducing puffiness and discomfort associated with swollen feet. Regular use of the tincture can provide relief from chronic foot swelling and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: St John's Wort contains compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling, which can help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet.
Vasodilatory effects: The herb's ability to dilate blood vessels can improve circulation and reduce pressure on the feet, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms like heaviness and fatigue.

Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and discomfort in swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it effective in relieving swelling in the feet.
Barks: The barks of St. John's Wort contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather hypericum perforatum dried flowers and leaves and ensure they are clean free of contaminants.
Combine one part dried hypericum perforatum with three parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to small amber glass bottles and store in a cool dark place indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock tinctures helps with swollen feet because it contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain. Regular use of herbal curly dock tincture can provide long-lasting relief from swollen feet, making it an effective natural remedy for this common issue.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of curly dock tincture help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort in the feet.
Wound-healing: Curly dock's wound-healing properties can help accelerate recovery from foot injuries or irritation, reducing swelling and promoting healthy tissue repair and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the curly dock plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes of curly dock contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh rumex crispus leaves and stems in the early morning for optimal potency.
Rinse the plant material with clean filtered water to remove dirt debris and impurities gently.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar leaving minimal space.
Cover the chopped plant material with 40 proof vodka filling the jar to the brim and secure tightly immediately.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily to promote extraction efficiently.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herb's mucilages work by forming a protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and relieving pressure. This allows for improved circulation and reduces fluid retention, leading to a decrease in swelling. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen feet, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating this common problem.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by suppressing prostaglandins, which cause inflammation.
Antioxidant: Marshmallow root is rich in antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic swelling and discomfort in the feet.

Demulcent: The mucilage content in marshmallow root forms a protective barrier on the skin, soothing and calming irritated tissues, and providing relief from itching, burning, and inflammation associated with swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe swelling in the feet.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold petals at dawn when dew is still present for optimal potency retention.
Rinse the harvested petals gently with filtered water and pat dry with paper towels immediately.
Combine one part of dried althaea officinalis root with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with swollen feet because of its natural diuretic properties, which encourage the body to release excess water and reduce fluid retention. The tannins present in fennel also help to strengthen blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing swelling. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet. As a result, herbal fennel tinctures can effectively alleviate symptoms of edema and leave feet feeling lighter, more comfortable, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet, making it an effective natural remedy for conditions like edema.
Diuretic: Fennel's diuretic effects help increase urine production, which can help flush out excess fluids and reduce fluid retention in the feet, thereby alleviating swelling.

Antioxidant: Fennel's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to chronic conditions like poor circulation and fluid buildup that can cause swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen feet.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making them effective in reducing swelling and relieving discomfort in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare herb and 40% ethanol vodka for the tincture preparation process.
Chop the dried foeniculum vulgare herb into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped herb with 100 milliliters of 40% ethanol vodka in a glass jar and stir well to ensure even coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with swollen feet because its cooling properties help to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the affected area. The menthol present in peppermint oil dilates blood vessels, allowing for better circulation and drainage of excess fluid. This natural diuretic effect helps to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with edema. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm irritated skin, providing fast relief from the aching sensation often experienced with swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues.
Pain-relieving: Peppermint contains compounds like menthol, which have a cooling effect on the skin and can help numb pain and discomfort, providing relief from swollen feet.
Antioxidant: Antioxidants in peppermint tinctures can help combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing swelling and promoting overall foot health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in swollen feet.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of peppermint contains menthol and other compounds that help to cool and soothe swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh mint leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier weighing approximately one pound.
Clean and dry the harvested mint leaves thoroughly using paper towels or a clean cloth to remove any dirt or moisture.
Chop the cleaned mint leaves into small pieces using sharp scissors or pruning shears to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Steep the chopped mint leaves in a glass jar filled with 100-proof vodka at a ratio of one pound of mint to two cups of vodka for six weeks allowing it to infuse and extract its medicinal properties.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the bottle with the preparation date and ingredients used for future reference.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for swollen feet are concentrated plant extracts that can be applied topically or taken orally to alleviate inflammation, pain, and discomfort associated with swelling. These natural remedies help by reducing fluid retention, improving circulation, and relaxing tense muscles. Examples of herbal tinctures effective in treating swollen feet include arnica, calendula, and ginger. By using these tinctures, individuals can experience relief from painful swelling, allowing them to enjoy daily activities without discomfort or limitation, such as taking a walk or wearing comfortable shoes.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen feet that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions such as arthritis, gout, and edema. Herbal tinctures like St. John's Wort, Ginger, and Turmeric have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain. Other herbs like Cayenne Pepper and Devil's Claw also ease joint discomfort and stiffness, providing relief from these similar ailments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with swollen feet is a blend of arnica, calendula, and witch hazel. Arnica reduces inflammation and relieves pain, while calendula promotes skin health and reduces swelling. Witch hazel, rich in tannins, helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup. This synergistic blend can be applied topically to the affected area, or taken sublingually in small amounts to promote overall circulation and reduce swelling. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Wounds : a compendium of clinical research and practice, herbal tinctures can be beneficial for swollen feet due to their wound healing properties. The study reviewed 14 clinical studies on the effects of medicinal plants in patients with diabetic foot ulcers and found that herbal medicines, administered as add-on therapy to standard wound care, significantly improved parameters such as ulcer width and depth, phagocytic function, tumor necrosis factor α level, epithelialization, vascularization, and wound closure. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting wound healing, herbal tinctures may help alleviate swelling in the feet.
